title: COMPRESSIBLE CFD: TRAPEZOIDAL FIN AERODYNAMICS
org: Advanced Fluid Dynamics, Propulsion, and Energy Lab (AFPEL)
what: Comparative CFD investigation of blunt-edged vs. streamlined trapezoidal rocket fin geometries at Mach 0.7 (Re = 1.58 × 10⁶) to quantify the drag–lift trade-off for mission-phase-specific selection
Full numerical study from mesh generation through post-processing — 5.88 million cell hexahedral domain, boundary layer refinement, and convergence to 10⁻⁶ residual tolerance
how: rhoSimpleFoam steady-state compressible RANS solver (OpenFOAM v2412) with Spalart-Allmaras single-equation turbulence model and 2nd-order linearUpwind discretization
Hex-dominant mesh with area-weighted y⁺ = 2.905 for the aero fin (viscous sublayer resolution), enabling direct skin friction calculation without wall functions
Intel Xeon HPC cluster 'Intruder' at Stony Brook University — ~8 core-hours (blunt) and ~432 core-hours (aero fin); ParaView and Python (NumPy, Matplotlib) for post-processing
results: 30.7% total drag reduction (0.02970 → 0.02057) with the streamlined aero fin — base separation wake isolated as 59.5% of blunt fin drag, directly targeting the dominant loss mechanism
62.4% lift penalty (L/D: 6.68 → 3.63) driven by negative viscous shear on the curved trailing edge — quantified the control-vs-efficiency trade-off for variable-phase flight design
Established geometry selection framework: blunt fin for control authority, streamlined fin for sustained high-speed efficiency; variable geometry recommended as hybrid solution
